facts.text: The name "Villas" comes from the Latin word for a country house, hinting at its early rural roots.
Lee County, where Villas sits, was carved out of Monroe County in 1887 to better serve residents far from Key West.
In 1881, industrialist Hamilton Disston bought four million acres, sparking canal and railroad projects that opened the area to settlers.
Villas shifted from a quiet agricultural outpost to a growing Fort Myers suburb as developers subdivided land for affordable single‑family homes.
As a census‑designated place, Villas can access federal programs, yet all taxes and ordinances remain under county control.
The community’s residents are 74.9% White, 5.8% Black or African American, 1.1% Asian, and 17.9% Hispanic or Latino, with 69.8% non‑Hispanic White.
